author: role.analyst.embodied-input

dimensionSummaries.dimension.player-fantasy: The player is a young knight whose physical gestures, growing fieldcraft, and repeated descents from a safe sky community gradually make him capable of protecting a close friend and founding a heroic legacy.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.core-loop: Prepare in the sky hub, choose a surface landing, traverse a dense puzzle-field, enter its dungeon, acquire or refine a capability, return upward to upgrade and socialize, then revisit a transformed region.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.verbs: Directional sword swings, thrust, shield bash, lock-on, stamina-limited sprint and climbing, sailcloth descent, dowsing, flying, aimed tools, object manipulation, and motion-shaped item use form an embodied vocabulary.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.world-state: Three surface regions acquire successive route and hazard variants, local time-shift fields replace selected present states with past states, and sky-surface links open through story and activated landmarks.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.topology: Aerial Skyloft and scattered sky islands form the service hub, while separated surface regions behave like dense obstacle courses whose familiar landforms are reused under changed conditions.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.gating-revisit: Story songs, dowsing targets, stamina routes, new tools, landing statues, local state devices, and upgraded equipment gate progress; revisits often use the same terrain with a new rule or objective.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.puzzle-information: Enemy guards, directional cuts, mechanism orientation, stamina paths, dowsing signals, local temporal boundaries, and tool-reactive surfaces provide explicit hypotheses and fast feedback.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.dungeon-structure: Distinct dungeons continue item-and-boss dependency structures while the surrounding surface itself adopts dungeon-like density, sequencing, and environmental rule escalation.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.exploration-reward: Insects, upgrade treasures, health pieces, goddess cubes and their sky rewards, side-quest tokens, bottles, and optional islands connect careful surface search to later hub benefits.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.growth-economy: Collected treasures and insects support equipment upgrades; wallet, item pouch, shield durability, ammunition, potions, health, and rupees make preparation and loadout management explicit.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.combat-boss: Enemies visibly guard directions, so sword angle, feint, distance, shield timing, stamina, and item choice matter; bosses escalate the same embodied read-and-response grammar.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.npc-quest: The sky settlement supports day-and-night routines, favors, shops, upgrades, and relationship episodes; a companion supplies analysis and dowsing while the central rescue relationship drives the route.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.narrative-performance: Frequent cinematics, repeated near-meetings, ritual trials, region transformations, and gesture-driven confrontations alternate authored drama with player-performed action.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.camera-blocking: Targeting stabilizes combat, first-person-like dowsing and aiming isolate a search direction, flight and descent cameras expose altitude, and staged cuts emphasize character and mechanism response.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.teaching-feedback: A long safe prologue teaches movement, flight, sword direction, social context, and interface; companion analysis, training rooms, optional hint media, telegraphs, and repeated landforms reinforce rules.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.ui-input: The Wii Remote Plus and Nunchuk are required; motion direction drives sword and several items, while radial selections, stamina, shield state, dowsing, and recalibration cues expose active mode.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.recovery-accessibility: Bird statues save and connect surface routes, stamina fruit restores traversal capacity, seats and consumables restore health, shields can be repaired or replaced, and optional hints reduce puzzle deadlock.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.perspective-implementation: Third-person 3D accommodates directional gesture combat, altitude, dense multi-level terrain, and local temporal overlays, but the original's limited free-camera control increases dependence on targeting and authored framing.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.hardware-multiplayer: The original is single-player and requires Wii MotionPlus capability plus a Nunchuk; physical calibration, player space, dominant-hand mapping, and gesture recognition are core design constraints.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.production-runtime: A faithful abstraction needs directional motion classification and calibration, target-relative guard states, stamina and durability, surface-state variants, local temporal volumes, hub-field transit, dowsing signals, upgrades, and cinematic handoff.

observationThemes.context: An enemy visibly guards one or more directions while remaining vulnerable from another angle.
preconditions: Motion input is calibrated well enough to distinguish attack directions.
playerInput: Read the guard, orient or feint the sword, then swing or thrust through an open line.
before: An undirected attack would strike the guard and create no opening.
action: Translate a chosen physical direction into a matching in-game attack vector.
after: The enemy blocks, evades, or takes damage according to the relation between guard and swing.
feedback: Enemy pose, sword orientation, impact sound, recoil, hit effect, and controller-to-animation correspondence expose the read.
consequences: Moment-to-moment offense becomes a cycle of observation, commitment, and correction rather than repeated generic attack input.
observationThemes.factualDescription: Wii MotionPlus maps several sword directions and a thrust, while many enemies alter their guard so the player must choose an exposed line.
observationThemes.interpretation: A high-dimensional input earns its complexity when opponent state visibly asks for one of those dimensions and gives immediate diagnostic feedback.

observationThemes.context: The player returns to a previously learned surface region for a later objective under changed hazards or traversal conditions.
preconditions: The region's base route is familiar.
A new story state, tool, song, or environmental condition is active.
playerInput: Re-enter through an unlocked landing and navigate by comparing remembered geometry with the new rule.
before: The player knows landmark relations but not the current dependency path.
action: Apply new capabilities and state reading to the stable landform.
after: Old routes change priority, new shortcuts or hazards become relevant, and a new objective path emerges.
feedback: Recognizable silhouettes, altered occupancy or material state, new targets, landing access, and objective signals expose continuity and change.
consequences: Spatial memory reduces reorientation while the new rule prevents the revisit from being a simple repeat.
observationThemes.factualDescription: The adventure sends the player back to the same three principal surface regions, where changed conditions and later capabilities produce different routes and challenges over familiar terrain.
observationThemes.interpretation: Reusing landforms can create density when every return changes the governing verb or state question rather than merely raising enemy strength.

observationThemes.context: A device creates a bounded temporal field in which terrain, machines, plants, enemies, or resources occupy a different historical state.
preconditions: A time-shift source can be activated or moved.
Relevant objects have authored present and past variants.
playerInput: Activate or reposition the field, observe which entities change, and exploit the temporarily valid route or function.
before: Present-state decay or terrain blocks access and disables needed mechanisms.
action: Move the boundary so selected objects enter their functioning past state.
after: Machinery operates, traversal geometry changes, enemies or resources transform, and the dependency becomes solvable within the active volume.
feedback: A sharp visible boundary, material and vegetation change, mechanical activation, sound transition, and object behavior identify temporal state.
consequences: One spatially local rule supports navigation, combat, resource, and puzzle combinations without resetting the whole map.
observationThemes.factualDescription: Selected desert mechanisms create local areas where nearby objects take their past forms, changing terrain, machinery, enemies, and usable routes while the effect is active.
observationThemes.interpretation: A bounded state field becomes generative when several entity classes share the rule and the player can manipulate the boundary itself.

observationThemes.context: The player activates an optional surface object whose reward is delivered to a corresponding location in the sky.
preconditions: The surface discovery has a linked aerial reward state.
playerInput: Activate the surface object, return upward, fly to the signaled island or chest, and claim the reward.
before: The optional reward is unavailable in the hub layer.
action: Create a cross-region link through exploration, then traverse the macro-map to complete it.
after: A sky reward becomes accessible and the player's inventory or capacity improves.
feedback: Activation scene, upward travel, map marker or visual signal, flight approach, and chest response connect the two locations.
consequences: Surface curiosity seeds a future reason to revisit the social and service hub instead of ending at the discovery site.
observationThemes.factualDescription: Activating goddess cubes on the surface unlocks corresponding treasure chests on sky islands, requiring a later flight to collect their contents.
observationThemes.interpretation: A delayed cross-map reward can braid exploration with hub return when both endpoints are clearly signaled and independently meaningful.

observationThemes.context: An enemy attack is incoming while the player's shield has finite durability.
preconditions: The enemy tell and impact window are visible.
playerInput: Choose passive guard or time a shield bash to the attack.
before: Taking the blow risks health, while passive defense consumes durability and may preserve enemy pressure.
action: Commit the defensive gesture inside or outside the counter window.
after: A successful bash interrupts or exposes the enemy; a weaker response spends durability or health and may require repair or replacement.
feedback: Shield meter or condition, impact sound, recoil, enemy stagger, controller motion, and shop repair state communicate the result.
consequences: Defense offers a skill-based momentum reversal while its durable resource cost keeps safer blocking meaningful.
observationThemes.factualDescription: Shields have durability and can block attacks, while a timed shield bash can deflect or stagger; damaged shields can be repaired, replaced, or upgraded.
observationThemes.interpretation: A recovery verb gains tactical depth when precise timing earns initiative and imprecise safety consumes a recoverable economy resource.

contributionHypotheses: Directional physical input can support readable combat when opponents visibly encode the required response dimension.
A compact set of landforms can feel dense when later visits replace the governing route rule rather than only repopulating content.
A movable local state field may generate puzzles by transforming several entity classes through one visible boundary.
uniqueOrNonportable: The exact sky-and-surface mythology, named island and regions, characters, sword origin, companion, rituals, devices, and story sequence are recognizable authored surface content and must not be reused.
Specific motion gestures, enemy guards, time-shift object designs, dungeon layouts, boss sequences, cube-to-chest placements, and audiovisual motifs are nonportable.
Wii MotionPlus calibration and required controller arrangement are historical platform expressions; portable patterns must specify alternate accessible inputs.
openQuestions: Skyward Sword HD's button-control, camera, hint, and pacing changes require a separate variant delta before accessibility conclusions include that edition.
A primary trace should distinguish truly persistent region changes from scenario-local variants before reuse rules are encoded.
